raw_data_source: Bi-national prospective cohort combining the CHANCE study at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center (US, 2015-2021) and the CHANCE Korea twin cohort at Samsung Medical Center (2018-2022), with standardized trichoscopy and the validated CADS distress scale.
Korean National Health Insurance Service (K-NHIS) nationwide database 2015-2023, with 1:3 propensity-score matching (5113 AR vs 14 738 IBR among 24 930 eligible patients).

Prospective single-center cohort at a tertiary cancer center from 2016, with 252 histologically confirmed SGC patients followed for up to 5 years using EORTC QLQ-C30 and H&N35 questionnaires; group-based trajectory analysis applied.

Korean National Health Insurance Service (K-NHIS) mother-child paired cohort 2005-2019 with follow-up through 2020; 5 461 222 live births, 3 640 815 unique mothers, with 1:4 propensity-score matching (145 315 ASCVD vs 581 260 non-ASCVD).
JACC editorial commentary on a target trial emulation study of statins in type 1 diabetes; no primary data source.

Samsung Medical Center Clinical Data Warehouse (CDW), 2002-2023, with 9565 Korean patients prescribed olanzapine; low-dose (<=1.25 mg/day) vs regular-dose comparison using time-varying exposure and age-as-timescale Cox models.

BIG-S prospective single-center cohort at Samsung Medical Center, recruiting newly diagnosed breast cancer patients from November 2018 (N=2,749); integrates EMR clinical data, bioelectrical impedance body composition, ICHOM-recommended PROs, and healthcare utilization records.
Single-arm prospective trial of scalp cooling in anthracycline-treated breast cancer patients (Korea); no PMC full text available beyond title/keywords.
Korean National Health Insurance Service (K-NHIS) nationwide cohort 2010-2020 linked to biennial national health screenings; 135,877 adults with pre- and post-PCI LDL-C and 7.4-year median follow-up.
European Heart Journal editorial/comment on an observational study of electronic cigarettes and cardiovascular outcomes; no primary data.

Circulation research letter reporting a Korean nationwide cohort study (likely K-NHIS) of newborns with congenital heart disease and their mothers; no PMC full text and no abstract available, so detail is limited to title.

Two-phase instrument development per COSMIN and US FDA PROM guidance: qualitative interviews (n=25) and cognitive interviews (n=15) for content validation, followed by a cross-sectional survey at one tertiary hospital and two local clinics in Korea (n=198) with EFA, IRT-GRM, CFA, and test-retest analyses.
Nationwide Korean claims database of AMI patients undergoing PCI with DAPT (ClinicalTrials.gov NCT06241833)
European Heart Journal editorial commentary (no primary data)
Korean National Health Insurance nationwide cohort of 17,973 post-PCI smokers with pre- and post-PCI health screening (ClinicalTrials.gov NCT06338761)

Two-phase study at a Korean tertiary university hospital: Phase I cognitive interviews (n=8) and Phase II psychometric validation (n=180) at Samsung Medical Center Breast Cancer Center outpatient clinic (Apr-Aug 2023)

Open-label 2:1 randomized controlled trial in newly diagnosed stage I-III breast cancer patients receiving neoadjuvant or adjuvant chemotherapy (Dec 2020-Aug 2021), with hair thickness and density measured by Folliscope 5.0 and CIA-related distress measured with CADS
Retrospective cohort of 4,880 newly diagnosed cancer patients screened with tablet/kiosk-based distress assessment at a university-affiliated cancer center in Seoul, South Korea (Jul 2014-Dec 2017)

Comprehensive Young Age Breast Cancer (CHARM) registry at Samsung Medical Center (since April 2013); patients <=40 at diagnosis or pregnant/postpartum within 1 year, with electronic medical record data, Medidata Rave EDC, central pathology review, annual bone density, multi-omics tumor/blood samples, reproductive hormone panels (AMH, estradiol, FSH, LH), and mobile PRO questionnaires
Samsung Medical Center Young Breast Cancer Registry (NCT03131089), 2013-2021; 610 patients <=40 years with FACT-G Social/Family Well-Being measured before and during treatment
Samsung Medical Center Young Breast Cancer Registry (NCT03131089), 2013-2021; 977 premenopausal patients <=40 years with FACT-ES at diagnosis

Development set: Breast Cancer Information Grand Round for Survivorship (BIG-S) cross-sectional study at Samsung Medical Center (2018-2019, n=6,265); external validation set: Beauty Education for Distressed Breast Cancer (BEST) longitudinal cohort at two Seoul university-based hospitals (2011-2016, n=432)

Cross-sectional survey of 4,366 adult breast cancer survivors who completed active treatment without recurrence, collected at breast cancer outpatient clinics in South Korea (November 2018 to April 2019)

Cross-sectional tablet-based PRO survey of 1,352 outpatients receiving chemotherapy and/or radiation therapy at the National Cancer Center (Goyang) and Samsung Medical Center (Seoul) between December 2017 and January 2018, using 10 cancer-specific PRO-CTCAE-Korean subsets developed via Delphi
Prospective head and neck cancer registry (2016-2020) of 225 SGC patients and 912 oral cavity/oropharyngeal cancer controls; EORTC QLQ-C30 and H&N35 modules (NCT02546895).

Multi-center two-arm RCT in Seoul and Suwon (Oct 2018-Feb 2020; CRIS KCT0003262); 239 newly diagnosed working-age cancer patients randomized 1:1 to START vs control, stratified by hospital, cancer type, and job collar.

Cross-sectional multi-center survey of 730 working-age (20-65) cancer patients who were working at diagnosis, recruited from Samsung Medical Center (Seoul) and Chonnam National University Hospital (Oct 2017-Mar 2018); Korean Occupational Stress Scale and Work and Meaning Inventory.

Nationwide retrospective cohort using Korean National Health Insurance Service (NHIS) claims and biennial health screening exam data (2002-2016): 84,969 incident breast cancer cases vs 1,057,674 age- and region-matched cancer-free controls; breast cancer treated as a time-varying exposure.

Cross-sectional validation study of 949 Korean NSCLC survivors (post-curative pulmonary resection, treatment ended >=1 month prior) recruited at Samsung Medical Center (Jan-Oct 2020); CaSUN-K, EORTC QLQ-C30, and K-FCRI-SF; EFA, CFA, Cronbach's alpha, convergent and known-group validity.
Multicenter cross-sectional survey of 727 working-age cancer survivors working at diagnosis (2017-2018) with measures of OFB, SFD, fear of cancer recurrence, spiritual well-being, and HRQoL.

Korean NHIS claims and health-screening data (2002-2016): 84,969 incident breast cancer cases vs 1,057,674 age- and region-matched controls, with breast cancer modeled as time-varying exposure and NHL subtype-specific outcomes (DLBCL, FL, mature T/NK-cell lymphomas, ALCL, others).

Cross-sectional validation survey of 188 CPD outpatients (tuberculosis, NTM, bronchiectasis, COPD, lung cancer, ILD, asthma) at Samsung Medical Center (Sep-Oct 2018); K-PROMIS-29 V2.1, SF-36v2, CAT, and mMRC; EFA, CFA, internal consistency, convergent/discriminant validity.
Randomized controlled clinical trial in breast cancer patients receiving chemotherapy, comparing a tailored moisturizer with a control (full abstract not available).

Retrospective longitudinal cohort of women aged >30 who underwent serial screening mammography at baseline and after menopause at Samsung Medical Center; mammographic breast density classified by ACR BI-RADS; 18,615 person-years of follow-up (median 4.8 years).

Unblinded RCT of 109 women newly diagnosed with stage I-III breast cancer (2014-2015) randomized to a 4-week mind-and-body education program (n=54) or control (n=55); outcomes assessed post-intervention and at 6 months (KCT0001191).
Cross-sectional survey of 349 cancer outpatients at Samsung Medical Center Comprehensive Cancer Center (Sep-Oct 2018) completing K-PROMIS-29 V2.1 and EORTC QLQ-C30
Author correction notice referring to the original Scientific Reports article (PMID 31913326) on the Kangbuk Samsung Cohort Study
Cross-sectional survey of 163 post-HSCT patients recruited from three university-based HSCT outpatient clinics and the Korea Blood Cancer Association, using Body Image Scale, WHOQOL-BREF, and PHQ-9
PRISMA-guided systematic search of PubMed, Cochrane Library, PsycINFO, and Embase (2009-2018) yielding 45 included studies; risk of bias assessed with ROBINS-I
Cross-sectional analysis of 171,321 participants in the Kangbuk Samsung Cohort Study (2011-2013) with PSI questionnaires and abdominal ultrasound

Double-blind randomized controlled pilot trial of 35 breast cancer survivors with PCIA at Samsung Comprehensive Cancer Center (Feb-Dec 2016); hair density and thickness measured by noninvasive bioengineering device

61 stage I-III breast cancer patients receiving AC, FAC, or AC-docetaxel adjuvant chemotherapy at Samsung Medical Center (Feb 2012-Jul 2013), followed pre-chemo, after 2 cycles, at 1/3/6 months and 3 years post-chemo with objective hair density and thickness measurements

Add-on study to the Samsung Medical Center Lymphoma Cohort Study (SMC-LCS): 370 NHL survivors (273 aggressive, 97 indolent) with baseline HRQoL (EORTC QLQ-C30) at diagnosis (2008-2011) and follow-up HRQoL plus QOL-CS at >=3 years (2014-2015)

Cross-sectional survey (May-Dec 2010) of 138 active-treatment and 128 posttreatment breast cancer patients from 23 Korean hospitals and 315 age- and area-matched general-population controls; measures included visual analogue scales and EORTC BR-23
Cross-sectional study of 283 nonmetastatic breast cancer survivors who completed treatment within 1 year and questionnaires (Subjective Happiness Scale, EORTC QLQ-C30) 2 years after diagnosis

Prospective cohort of 61 stage I-III postoperative breast cancer patients receiving adjuvant chemotherapy at a Korean outpatient breast cancer clinic (Feb-Sep 2012), with objective skin measurements via noninvasive bioengineering device at pre-chemo, after 2 cycles, and 1/3/6 months post-completion
